collective.playertracking.message.cannot: You cannot track all with this type of tracker
collective.playertracking.message.explan: For explanation on '/track all' read page 4.
collective.playertracking.message.ifluck: If you are lucky enough to find a player on 2 different arms, it's time to lower the size of your arms. Keep moving the Gold block down/up your arm to pinpoint the location of the player you want to track. If he doesn't show up anymore after you moved the Gold block, place the block higher up until you are at a 1 block difference. Now all that is left is to add the length of the arm from the respective coordinate on your Diamond block and you've found the location of an enemy within 25 blocks!
collective.playertracking.message.inform: For information on building a tracker read page 3.
collective.playertracking.message.introd: For an introduction to tracking read page 2.
collective.playertracking.message.needso: You need to be on a solid tracker to '/track all'
collective.playertracking.message.needtr: You need to be on a tracking block to do that.
collective.playertracking.message.oncema: Once you have made your own solid tracker, it's time to find those bases! Stand on the Diamond block in the middle of your tracker and do /track all. If there are players within the range of your tracker they will show up. You will need a player on at least 2 of your arms to continue to page 5. If not, try to increase the length of your arms or wait until there are more/different players online.
collective.playertracking.message.pictur: In the picture below you can see multiple letters and dashes. The dashes are Obsidian blocks, D is a Diamond block, and G are Gold blocks.
collective.playertracking.message.procee: To proceed to page 5 do '/track help 5'
collective.playertracking.message.seelay: To see a 'layout' of a tracker do '/track help 3 layout'
collective.playertracking.message.startb: To start building a solid tracker you will need a 'Diamond Block' for the middle, 4 'Gold Blocks' for the end of each 'arm', and as much 'Obsidian' as you can find for the length of the arms. Every obsidian block equals 25 blocks in the real world. Build your tracker at a smart position. Not too close to spawn, and not at coords that are easy to guess. You are not the only one that will try to make one.
collective.playertracking.message.tracki: Tracking is a feature to encourage raiding on a server. Without this it would be near-impossible to find enemy bases legit, and would give hackers an unfair advantage. Tracking is the art of finding a player before he or she finds you. It is not cheap, you will need many valuable materials to start off. Read the next few pages for more information.
collective.playertrackingcompass.message: Unable to redirect the compass. There are no players around or they're too close.

collective.voidvanguards.message.origina: Original location is not in save data.
collective.voidvanguards.message.skyship: You receive a glimps of something high above, it seems to be in a stable orbit around the world.
Maybe you could look at it closer with a spyglass?
You suddenly see an explosion of energy eminating from the ship!
Everything feels normal again. The object must have left its orbit.
As you lose focus of the sky ship, the details have been lost.
The closer you look at the ship, the more details are revealed.
Sky ship visibility has been reset.
%s has witnessed an explosion in the sky!
collective.voidvanguards.message.unabler: Unable to return player to original position.
collective.voidvanguards.message.vanguar: Hello %s.
...are we losing %s...
...NO IT CAN'T... ...DEFENSIVE POSITIONS...
...BEHIND Y...
We have been observing your activity from orbit and are aware that you have noticed us.
Oh that explosion? Don't worry, we all made it out safely. But it's one of the reasons you're here.
You are in our headquarters, a couple of light-minutes away from the place you call home.
But an introduction might be in order.
We are the Vanguards. Explorers of realms and void. Defenders of time and space.
The explosion you witnessed was part of the ongoing effort from our adversaries to destroy us.
It did not work, as you can see, but the %s will not stop until we have seized to exist.
What we need from you is to...
You look around. You're back where you were before.
You must destroy the base located at:
%s
When you are ready, go to these coordinates:
%s
Enter the portal. Destroy the radar on the edge of the base.
Be careful. I will see you once the mission is complete.
Good luck. Over and out.
Are you safe?
I'm glad I was able to get you away from the station.
We were attacked. It has been rough..
Time moves differently in the cosmos. A week has passed since we met.
There are.. less of us now. We were able to bury our dead.
I have a favour to ask.
We've detected a forwarding base operated by supporters of %s.
I can't risk teleporting again until that base is inoperable.
%s, you did it.
With the radar destroyed, we were able to penetrate %s's defenses.
I'm glad you didn't fall for their false promises.
This has been an enormously important step in halting their advance of eternal darkness.
We'll handle things from here, I’m sending you back to the cosmos portal.
Thank you for your bravery, I will contact you soon.
You've done us and the world a great service.
With the %s threat taken care of, for now, we're moving on to a different quadrant.
I'd like to give you this:
> You received an Orbital Transponder
It grants you the ability to teleport between this planet and the Vanguard Station at will.
Remodel it if you like, but keep it functional. We might need it again.
Good luck on your future ventures.
Commander %s signing out.
collective.voidvanguards.message.voidbor: AGH. You have chosen violence.
So be it. May this place be the death of you.
Voidborn! Attack!
~ Having turned down the offer, you'll still need to find the radar and disable it! ~
Some magical force is preventing you from breaking blocks.
Some magical force is preventing you from placing blocks.
You flip the lever, ignoring every safety protocol. The radar sizzles as you force a hard shutdown.
The radar is broken and the lever is stuck.
! Radar control
must be kept in
upper position
at all times !
That familiar feeling of being warped fills your mind.
As you enter the portal, you feel your existance warping through space and time.
Some magical force is preventing you from entering the portal.
The existing voidborn around the portal prevent you from entering!
You're back in the voidborn asteroid base.
Hoping you made the right decision.
You've made the right choice.
Give me a moment to trace their location.
I shall initiate the teleport soon.
Let's go, shall we?
Stop! Please stop!
All you need to do is hand me that radio.
~ You can choose to attack, or right-click the Orbital Radio on the Voidborn Leader. The choice is yours. ~
You have proven that you are capable enough.
Killing us will accomplish nothing.
We are born from the void, and will rise again.
However, if we can come to a certain agreement, we can both benefit from this situation.
I can sense traces of an Orbital Radio on you.
With that, I can track the Vanguard's location.
...and we can get rid of them.
This base, the asteroid and all its riches will be yours.
%s? What are you doing here?
We'll take it from here.
Enjoy the asteroid.
Has the Voidborn radar been destroyed?
How.. did you get here?
... no it can't be ...
Hello Lyra.
That radio was a very useful piece of equipment.
It has been a pleasure being your adversary.
Now it's time to end this.
Thank you, %s.
collective.voidvanguards.vanguard.name: Vanguard
collective.voidvanguards.voidborn.name: Voidborn
item.voidvanguards.orbital_radio.descrip: Could this be used to contact the Vanguard?
item.voidvanguards.orbital_radio.lore: A high-tech radio that hums faintly.
item.voidvanguards.orbital_radio.pickedu: As you pick up the radio, it starts vibrating. You're not entirely sure if you should try to answer it.
item.voidvanguards.orbital_radio.spawned: Suddenly, a strange radio materializes in mid-air behind you!
Suddenly, a strange radio materializes in mid-air before you!
item.voidvanguards.orbital_radio.useUnab: You are unable to get a signal.
item.voidvanguards.orbital_transponder.a: You feel the item vibrate and hum!
item.voidvanguards.orbital_transponder.l: You leave the station, and materialize back on the planet.
A beacon that locks onto the Vanguard Station.
item.voidvanguards.orbital_transponder.u: You successfully beam on to the Vanguard Station.
You are unable to get a signal.
